_note: v1 elasticities are hand-calibrated from public literature (IMF WEO ch.1 energy pass-through, BIS cross-border credit, World Bank Commodity Markets Outlook). Units: for each shock, delta_risk_points = elasticity * magnitude, where magnitude is the user-facing number (0.20 for +20% oil, 1.0 for +100bp, 0.10 for +10% DXY, -2.0 for -2pp China GDP). Elasticities are sign-preserving: positive = risk rises when the shock variable rises. Replace with VAR/LP IRFs post-MVP once a 10y+ panel is cached.

shocks.label: Oil +20%
shocks.description: Brent/WTI +20%. Commodity-importer EU/UK hit hardest; oil-exporters (CO, BR, MX, US) benefit.

shocks.label: Fed +100bp
shocks.description: US 10Y / fed funds +100bp. Propagates to EM via USD funding stress.

shocks.label: Fed -50bp
shocks.description: Fed easing cycle; EM benefits, DM mildly supportive.

shocks.label: USD +10%
shocks.description: Broad DXY strength. EM FX/inflation/debt service all worse.

shocks.label: China GDP -2pp
shocks.description: China growth slowdown. LatAm commodity exporters and DE (machinery/autos) hit hardest.
